entities.name: 基础模型 (foundation models)
entities.how_described: 本集核心议题之一：模型可能会面临持续的激烈竞争，最终沦为像电信运营商一样低利润、无差异的基础设施大宗商品；由于缺乏赢家通吃效应，模型公司难以拥有长期的定价权，真正价值将向应用层转移。

evidence.quote: And even if we do end up with, say, pick a number, three to six to 10 giant foundation models that cost hundreds of billions of dollars a year, fine, do they get all the value from that?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And so this is the elemental question right now around foundation models is, does the model do the whole thing?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 定价权 (pricing power)
entities.how_described: 本集反复质疑模型实验室是否能拥有无限的定价权：如果模型本身缺乏根本差异化且面临无限竞争，最终就只能以边际成本销售大宗商品，从而失去高利润率与定价权。

evidence.quote: And do the model labs have pricing power?

evidence.quote: And so it does sort of seem to me that if the chatbot isn't the UX, and it needs to be apps, and the model companies aren't going to build that, and the models themselves are basically commodities, at least as you can see them as users, then why would the model companies have pricing power?

evidence.quote: So we should presume we don't know, but they're all this sort of basic building blocks of like, well, but why would they have pricing power?

evidence.quote: Obviously this is a whole, everyone is asking like, I'm not sure how many people are asking whether model labs have pricing power.

entities.name: 前向部署工程师 (forward deployed engineers)
entities.how_described: 本集将其比喻为在旧金山工作的埃森哲外包软件开发者；指出连 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 都在斥巨资投资这类工程师和咨询服务，因为重构企业的内部工作流和落地系统需要大量人力定制。

evidence.quote: Along those lines, something you've been writing a bit about is, this unexpected investment in professional services/consulting services/forward deployed engineers, all the AI labs, at least the two big ones, OpenAI and Anthropic, are investing in buying massive consultancies and PE firms.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 企业软件销售周期 (enterprise software sales cycle)
entities.how_described: 本集指出，大公司买 ChatGPT 然后两周开除所有员工是不懂真实世界运作：企业软件的销售周期极其漫长，通常需要 18 个月甚至更久才能谈成一笔交易。

evidence.quote: Turn to a big company, enterprise software sales cycle, you'll know this better than me, enterprise software sales cycle is like 18 months, if you're lucky.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 杰文斯悖论 (Jevons paradox)
entities.how_described: 本集解释为一种价格弹性：当技术进步使得写代码或做分析的成本变便宜时，市场对这些任务的需求会呈爆炸式增长，最终反而需要更多人去做更高阶的判断和工作。

evidence.quote: What happens much more, and this is why people talked about the Jevons paradox is this pricey elasticity, because the Jevons paradox is just price elasticity, applied price elasticity.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 劳动总量谬误 (lump of labor fallacy)
entities.how_described: 本集用来反驳「AI 会导致人类工作彻底消失」的观点：过去 200 年里，每一项新技术都会消除一堆工作、创造一堆新工作并解锁新价值，虽然经历时伴随痛苦，但最终我们都会更富有。

evidence.quote: I think the other answer, it's back to the lump of labor fallacy, and the last 200 years, that each of these technologies removes a bunch of jobs, creates a bunch of new jobs, creates a bunch of new value, unlocks prosperity for all of us, and that's painful as you go through it, but it always creates more value.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 大宗商品 (commodity)
entities.how_described: 本集将基础模型和电信运营商网络比作大宗商品：它们虽然技术极其复杂、建设成本极高，但品质缺乏明显差异，消费者不愿为其支付品牌溢价，因此利润率会被极度挤压。

evidence.quote: And the stocks have gone nowhere in 25 years, because it's an X gross low margin commodity utility, where they're selling this objectively amazing piece of global technology infrastructure that has enormous complexity and enormous sophistication, but all the cool stuff is made by you.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Aren't you basically, have you got like three to six companies selling a commodity at marginal cost?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I think that's the crux of this, is they're undifferentiated commodity infrastructure providers.

entities.name: 深度伪造 (deep fake)
entities.how_described: 本集将其视为 AI 技术的阴暗面之一：虽然以前用 Photoshop 也能造假，但以前 15 岁的小孩没法在一个下午给全校同学伪造出不雅图片并发送出去，现在 AI 让这种危害变得极其容易。

evidence.quote: If you think about, for example, the deep fake needs issue, for example, there's like a dumb reaction to this, which is to say, "Haven't you heard of Photoshop?"
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Deep fake news are like the obvious thing we can see now.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 根本的不确定性 (radical uncertainty)
entities.how_described: 本集坚持的核心观点：面对 AI 和 AGI，我们没有关于人类智能的理论，也没有模型为何有效的理论，一切预测都是凭感觉；因此普通人必须拥抱不确定性，亲自下场试。

evidence.quote: Coming back to this phrase you use, presume radical uncertainty is a nice core thesis here.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Mac minis
entities.how_described: 本集提到科技圈里有极少数极其硬核的人购买组建了 Mac mini 集群，完全不用 Google 了，以此作为「采用程度参差不齐」的极端案例。

evidence.quote: So there's people in tech who bought their cluster of Mac minis, and don't use Google anymore.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: A16Z
entities.how_described: 本集提到 Benedict Evans 曾是该机构的长期合伙人与内部分析师，以及他在该机构工作时研究过一家叫 Frame.io 的视频协作公司。

evidence.quote: Benedict was a longtime partner at A16Z as their in house analyst and resident thinker.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: I know you didn't actually do investing at A16C even though you work for A16Z.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: While Steven Jay Sinofsky at A16Z who used to run Windows would always say, "Incumbents always try and make the new thing a feature."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Bain
entities.how_described: 本集作为顶级战略管理咨询公司的代表被提及：AI 落地需要企业去重构内部工作流，而大公司并没有闲人来做这个，这正是 Bain 等咨询公司存在的意义。

evidence.quote: All of those kinds of questions are reasons why you hire Bain, BCG, McKinsey on one side, or Accenture, Infosys, whoever on the other, or you hire a branding agency, or you hire an arch firm of architects or whatever.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And on the other side, that's why you hire, depending on what you're trying to do, you hire Bain, or you hire Accenture, or you hire publicists to help you work that out.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: What you actually pay Bain to do is to go and walk all over your company and work out.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: BCG
entities.how_described: 本集作为顶级战略管理咨询公司的代表被提及：指出企业雇佣 BCG 是为了帮他们走遍全公司、调研客户需求并弄清楚如何重构内部工作流。

evidence.quote: All of those kinds of questions are reasons why you hire Bain, BCG, McKinsey on one side, or Accenture, Infosys, whoever on the other, or you hire a branding agency, or you hire an arch firm of architects or whatever.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Accenture
entities.how_described: 本集将其作为大型 IT 服务与外包企业的代表，指出将垂直系统接入水平系统并培训员工是另一个大工程，这正是雇佣 Accenture 来做落地改造的原因。

evidence.quote: And there's something in there of like a forward deployed engineer is like an Accenture outsource software developer who lives in San Francisco, or works in San Francisco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: All of those kinds of questions are reasons why you hire Bain, BCG, McKinsey on one side, or Accenture, Infosys, whoever on the other, or you hire a branding agency, or you hire an arch firm of architects or whatever.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And on the other side, that's why you hire, depending on what you're trying to do, you hire Bain, or you hire Accenture, or you hire publicists to help you work that out.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Infosys
entities.how_described: 本集将其与 Accenture 并列，作为大型 IT 服务与外包企业代表，指出它们承担了企业接入新系统、构建新工作流并培训员工的繁杂落地工作。

evidence.quote: All of those kinds of questions are reasons why you hire Bain, BCG, McKinsey on one side, or Accenture, Infosys, whoever on the other, or you hire a branding agency, or you hire an arch firm of architects or whatever.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Uber
entities.how_described: 本集作为被互联网彻底重塑的行业案例：在 1997 年看互联网完全预测不到它会摧毁出租车业务，但它不仅把市场做大了，而且让所有人都转投了过去。

evidence.quote: So, if we'd been having this conversation in 1997, it's like the Uber test.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The other side of this, of course, is this is one of the charts at the end of my presentation is comparing Uber and Airbnb, because this is like the cliche from Mark Andreessen that Uber doesn't sell software to taxi companies, Airbnb doesn't sell software to hotels.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Well, a whole bunch of cities were Uber demolished taxi business, and made it much bigger as well.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Airbnb
entities.how_described: 本集将其与 Uber 的市场影响作对比：Airbnb 开辟了全新的业务，但对传统酒店业的影响其实相当微小，因为酒店业务有一半是极其刚需的商务旅行。

evidence.quote: And that's a way that you could look at Google, or the way you could look at Airbnb or all these kinds of companies.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And right now, those questions, you only even know what the question is after it's been asked and you've built a billion dollar thing that lots of people use because obviously Spotify look crazy, and people look crazy, and Airbnb look crazy.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Netscape
entities.how_described: 本集指出，当马克·安德森在 93、94 年推出 Netscape 时，全球只有 5000 万到 1 亿台 PC，但由于站在了前人硬件和宽带的肩膀上，互联网的普及速度得以大大加快。

evidence.quote: When Mark Andreessen launched Netscape in what was it, '93, '94, there were like 50 to 100 million PCs on earth.

entities.name: VisiCalc
entities.how_described: 本集将其作为绝佳比喻：它是 70 年代末最早的电子表格软件，改变一个利率其他数字瞬间全变，但对当时的律师和记者毫无影响；以此类比今天 AI 之于不同行业的「顿悟时刻」差异。

evidence.quote: And so that's sort of the moment that we're in of, there's some people like software developers are the accountant seeing VisiCalc.
